codes.description: codeprobe could not auto-detect whether the repository's narrative source is GitHub PRs, GitLab MRs, or another backend.
codes.remediation_pattern: Pass --narrative-source=<github|gitlab|...> explicitly.

codes.description: Two mutually exclusive CLI flags were supplied on the same invocation.
codes.remediation_pattern: Remove one of the conflicting flags and retry.

codes.description: Trace capture exceeded the configured overflow threshold and was truncated.
codes.remediation_pattern: Re-run with --trace-max-bytes=<higher> or --trace-disable to avoid truncation.

codes.description: No experiment was found matching the current working directory or the user's selection history.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe experiment list` and `codeprobe experiment new` to establish an experiment.

codes.description: Multiple experiments match the current selection; codeprobe needs disambiguation.
codes.remediation_pattern: Pass --experiment=<id> with the exact experiment id from `codeprobe experiment list`.

codes.description: The selected experiment or suite contains no runnable tasks.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe experiment show` to inspect task inclusion rules, or mine/scaffold new tasks.

codes.description: The provided suite filter did not match any known suites.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe suite list` to see available suites and re-issue with a valid name.

codes.description: The provided --permission-mode value is not one of the accepted modes.
codes.remediation_pattern: Pass --permission-mode=<readonly|workspace|full> (see `codeprobe run --help`).

codes.description: The run was interrupted by the user (SIGINT/SIGTERM) before completion.
codes.remediation_pattern: Inspect partial results under .codeprobe/runs/<run-id> and re-run if desired.

codes.description: Per-task trace budget was exceeded; the task's trace was capped.
codes.remediation_pattern: Re-run with --trace-budget=<higher> or --no-trace to skip trace capture.

codes.description: Exporting source artifacts requires explicit acknowledgement of the data egress implications.
codes.remediation_pattern: Re-run with --acknowledge-source-export to confirm intent.

codes.description: The canary task failed its proof-of-work check, indicating an unreliable run environment.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor` and inspect canary artifacts under .codeprobe/runs/<run-id>/canary.

codes.description: Canary proof-of-work is required for this run configuration but was not enabled.
codes.remediation_pattern: Re-run with --canary-proof to enable canary verification.

codes.description: The canary result did not match the expected reference output.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor` and compare canary diff output to identify environment drift.

codes.description: The canary gate blocked the run because canary metrics fell outside acceptance bounds.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --canary` to review gate thresholds and canary deltas.

codes.description: Snapshot creation failed (disk, permissions, or git state).
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor` to diagnose the snapshot backend and retry after resolving the underlying issue.

codes.description: Snapshot integrity verification failed; the snapshot is corrupt or has drifted.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe snapshot verify --verbose` and rebuild the snapshot if necessary.

codes.description: Required metadata fields are missing from a task, run, or snapshot record.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe validate` on the affected artifact to identify missing fields.

codes.description: Metadata is present but violates schema (wrong type, out-of-range value, or malformed reference).
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe validate --strict` and repair the offending fields.

codes.description: Detected drift between recorded agent capability profile and current environment.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --capabilities` to view drift details and re-baseline if intentional.

codes.description: The requested backend identifier does not match any registered codeprobe backend.
codes.remediation_pattern: Pass --backend=<one of: claude|codex|copilot|...> — see `codeprobe run --help` for the current registry.

codes.description: No agent backends are configured or discoverable in this environment.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor` to see which backends were probed and install at least one.

codes.description: Offline preflight checks failed — required cached artifacts or local binaries are unavailable.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --offline` to list missing prerequisites before retrying.

codes.description: The supplied repository URL could not be parsed as a valid git URL.
codes.remediation_pattern: Pass --repo=<https://host/owner/name.git or git@host:owner/name.git> with a well-formed URL.

codes.description: git clone failed (network, auth, or server-side error).
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --net` and verify git credentials and connectivity before retrying.

codes.description: Calibration results were rejected because they fell outside acceptance bounds or statistical power thresholds.
codes.remediation_pattern: Inspect `codeprobe calibrate report` and increase sample size or adjust thresholds as appropriate.

codes.description: One or more `codeprobe doctor` checks failed.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --verbose` to view per-check failure details.

codes.description: The run exceeded its configured cost/time budget. This error is intentionally diagnostic (not prescriptive): auto-retrying with a higher budget is unsafe and must be an explicit human decision.
codes.remediation_pattern: Review the run's cost report and, if intentional, re-run with an explicitly increased --budget — but only after confirming the previous run's behaviour.

codes.description: codeprobe could not infer a goal or intent for mining from the repository signals available.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe mine --goals` with an explicit goal specification, or supply --goal=<description>.

codes.description: Sourcegraph-backed discovery exceeded the configured request or token quota.
codes.remediation_pattern: Inspect `codeprobe doctor --sg` output and wait for quota reset or reduce discovery scope.

codes.description: A component attempted network IO while codeprobe was running in --offline mode.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --offline` to identify the component and either pre-cache its data or drop --offline.

codes.description: Running in CI requires an explicit tenant binding; codeprobe refuses to infer one.
codes.remediation_pattern: Set the CODEPROBE_TENANT environment variable (or `--tenant=<id>`) in your CI configuration.

codes.description: The user-home skill bundle referenced by this run is older than the currently installed codeprobe version.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --skills` and re-install the referenced skill bundle to refresh it.

codes.description: The configured LLM provider is unavailable (auth, rate limit, or outage). Usually surfaced as a warning rather than a hard stop.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `codeprobe doctor --llm` and verify provider credentials and status.

codes.description: Another codeprobe process holds the tenant lock for the same command (mine/run/snapshot). Only one such invocation may run per tenant at a time.
codes.remediation_pattern: Run `ps -p <holder_pid>` to inspect the live holder, or remove the stale lockfile at ~/.codeprobe/state/{tenant}/.lock-<command> if you are sure the holder is dead.
